text.title: Headline Angles and Pinning Rules
text.summary: Ten headline angle categories with examples, keyword inclusion rules, and position pinning guidelines.

rule.statement: Cover at least 7 of the 10 angle categories across 15 headlines — heavy repetition of a single angle wastes slots and prevents the algorithm from identifying which message resonates.
rule.rationale: Google's RSA system tests combinations; angles that are nearly identical produce statistically indistinguishable results, providing no learning signal.

list.item: Keyword match — "Project Management Software" (exact search term)

rule.statement: Include the primary keyword in at least 2 headlines, placed naturally — do not keyword-stuff or truncate at character limit to fit the keyword.
rule.rationale: Keyword relevance in headlines directly improves Quality Score → lower CPC. Truncated or unnatural keyword placement degrades CTR, negating the Quality Score benefit.

rule.statement: Pin a headline to position 1 only when brand or legal compliance requires it to always appear — avoid pinning for performance reasons, as it reduces the combination space available to the algorithm.
rule.rationale: Each pinned position removes a testing slot from the algorithm. Pinning 3 headlines effectively converts the RSA into a fixed ETA, eliminating all adaptive optimization.
